risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Participants were then randomized either to topical 1% testosterone gel (10-g daily) or placebo gel daily in 1:1 allocation during a 14-week intervention period using concealed randomization and permuted blocks with a block size of 4." Comment: This describes a proper random allocation process. Permuted blocks with 1:1 allocation and concealed randomization are context clues indicating a truly random sequence, so Low risk is appropriate.

_evidence.method_quote: Participants were then randomized either to topical 1% testosterone gel (10-g daily) or placebo gel daily in 1:1 allocation during a 14-week intervention period using concealed randomization and permuted blocks with a block size of 4.
_evidence.method_summary: Participants were randomized 1:1 by concealed randomization using permuted blocks of size 4.

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Participants were then randomized either to topical 1% testosterone gel (10-g daily) or placebo gel daily in 1:1 allocation during a 14-week intervention period using concealed randomization and permuted blocks with a block size of 4." Comment: The article explicitly states that concealed randomization was used, which indicates the recruiter could not foresee the upcoming assignment before enrollment. Although the exact concealment mechanism is not further specified, the explicit statement of concealed randomization is sufficient for Low risk here.

_evidence.concealment_quote: Participants were then randomized either to topical 1% testosterone gel (10-g daily) or placebo gel daily in 1:1 allocation during a 14-week intervention period using concealed randomization and permuted blocks with a block size of 4.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The investigating staff and the participants were blinded to intervention allocation; the group and testosterone dose assignment was known only to the Investigational Drug Pharmacist, who dispensed testosterone gel and sildenafil, and a designated physician, who performed the dose titration. To maintain blinding, the participants received 3-tubes of the gel daily; those assigned to the 10-g dose received daily two tubes containing testosterone gel and one tube containing the placebo gel, while those assigned to placebo group received three tubes of the placebo gel." Comment: This directly states that both participants and investigating staff/personnel were blinded, with a masking procedure designed to maintain blinding. Because the study also included subjective outcomes (e.g. PGWBI, DABS, erectile function), successful blinding is important; the explicit blinding description supports a low risk judgement for performance bias.

_evidence.blinding_quote: "The investigating staff and the participants were blinded to intervention allocation; the group and testosterone dose assignment was known only to the Investigational Drug Pharmacist, who dispensed testosterone gel and sildenafil, and a designated physician, who performed the dose titration. To maintain blinding, the participants received 3-tubes of the gel daily; those assigned to the 10-g dose received daily two tubes containing testosterone gel and one tube containing the placebo gel, while those assigned to placebo group received three tubes of the placebo gel."
_evidence.blinding_method: participants received 3-tubes of the gel daily; those assigned to the 10-g dose received daily two tubes containing testosterone gel and one tube containing the placebo gel, while those assigned to placebo group received three tubes of the placebo gel

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The investigating staff and the participants were blinded to intervention allocation; the group and testosterone dose assignment was known only to the Investigational Drug Pharmacist, who dispensed testosterone gel and sildenafil, and a designated physician, who performed the dose titration." Comment: The study explicitly states that investigating staff were blinded, and the outcomes include patient-reported questionnaires (PGWBI, DABS) assessed in a trial described as double-blind. Because the participants were also blinded, the patient self-reported outcomes are unlikely to have been influenced by knowledge of allocation. The objective outcomes (serum testosterone, hematocrit, PSA, prostate exams) were also not dependent on subjective judgment in a way that would create detection bias. Overall, this supports low risk for blinding of outcome assessment.

_evidence.assessor_blinding_quote: The investigating staff and the participants were blinded to intervention allocation; the group and testosterone dose assignment was known only to the Investigational Drug Pharmacist, who dispensed testosterone gel and sildenafil, and a designated physician, who performed the dose titration.

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "140 men were randomized either to sildenafil and testosterone gel (n=70) or sildenafil and placebo gel (n=70). In the testosterone arm, 5 were lost to follow-up, 2 withdrew due to skin irritation, 2 were discontinued due to developing hematocrit exceeding 54%, and 1 withdrew due to the smell of the gel. In the placebo arm, 11 were lost to follow up and 1 withdrew due to skin irritation. Thus, 60 and 58 participants in the testosterone and placebo arms, respectively, completed the trial." Comment: Post-randomization attrition on the analysed outcome is reported with per-arm numbers. Loss was moderate overall (22/140; 15.7%) and balanced across arms (70 randomized vs 60 analysed in testosterone; 70 vs 58 in placebo; differential 2.9 pp). Reasons were mostly loss to follow-up or non-outcome-related tolerability/safety issues, and the study additionally used multiple imputation for missing PGWBI/DABS data, which supports low risk of attrition bias.

per_arm_flow.reasons: 5 were lost to follow-up, 2 withdrew due to skin irritation, 2 were discontinued due to developing hematocrit exceeding 54%, and 1 withdrew due to the smell of the gel.
11 were lost to follow up and 1 withdrew due to skin irritation.

_evidence.notes: Attrition numbers are for the randomized trial; the article also reports 12 men were not randomized during the sildenafil optimization phase, but those are pre-randomization exclusions.
